body:
Hey, I was going over the notes from Tuesday's call and I'm still not totally clear on the rollback window for the auth cutover. Are we committing to a hard cutover on Friday night or is there a 24-hour parallel-run period before we fully deprecate the old tokens?

Also, did you loop in Devon yet? He's the one who set up the original OAuth flow and I'd feel better if he signed off before we pull the trigger. Last thing we need is to break SSO the week before the Hartwell demo.

- Matt
snippet: Hey, I was going over the notes from Tuesday's call and I'm still not totally cl...
subject: auth cutover plan

body:
Yes, we're doing a 24-hour parallel run starting Friday at 11pm — both token systems will be live simultaneously so we can monitor error rates before the old one goes dark Saturday night. I added that to the runbook this morning, I'll send you the doc link once I clean it up.

And yes, Devon is looped in. He actually pushed a small fix to the token refresh logic yesterday so we're in better shape than we were Monday. I do want to do one more dry run in staging tomorrow afternoon if you're free around 3 — it'll take maybe 45 minutes.

- Steph
snippet: Yes, we're doing a 24-hour parallel run starting Friday at 11pm — both token sys...
subject: Re: auth cutover plan

body:
3pm tomorrow works, I'll block it now. Send me the staging environment credentials when you get a chance — I think mine expired last week and I never renewed them.

Good to hear Devon got that refresh bug sorted. I'd also suggest we set up a Slack alert on the old token endpoint so we know the moment traffic drops to zero — cleaner than watching logs manually at midnight. We can call that the official cutover confirmation and both go to sleep at a reasonable hour for once.

- Matt
snippet: 3pm tomorrow works, I'll block it now. Send me the staging environment credentia...
subject: Re: auth cutover plan

body:
Hey, so I've been looking at flights to LA and the prices are getting a bit crazy. There's a decent option leaving Friday the 27th at 6:15am out of SFO that gets us into Burbank by 7:45am, which would give us the whole day with Tara. The other option is Thursday evening but I know you have that call with the Singapore team that might run late.

Also — did you ever hear back from Tara about whether we can crash there Saturday night, or should I just book the Airbnb I had bookmarked? It's $180/night which isn't terrible.

Let me know what you think. I can book tonight if we decide.
snippet: Hey, so I've been looking at flights to LA and the prices are getting a bit craz...
subject: Trip logistics - need to finalize a few things

body:
The Friday morning flight works better for me. The Thursday call is already looking like it'll go until at least 8pm so I'd be a mess trying to get to the airport after that. Go ahead and book the 6:15am — I'll set three alarms, I promise.

I texted Tara last night and she said we're totally welcome Saturday. She has a pull-out couch in the guest room so we'd have actual privacy which is nice. That saves us the $180 so maybe we put that toward the dinner in Silver Lake we've been talking about?

Also, can you double-check that your hiking boots still fit okay? Last time you said the left one was giving you blisters and I don't want a repeat of the Sedona situation.
snippet: The Friday morning flight works better for me. The Thursday call is already look...
subject: Re: Trip logistics - need to finalize a few things

body:
Flights are booked! Confirmation number is XHQK72, I forwarded the email to you too. And yes, dinner in Silver Lake is a great call — I'll make a reservation for Saturday around 7:30, that should give us enough time to clean up after the hike.

I tried on the boots last night and honestly they're fine on the right but the left one is still a little tight at the toe. I might just grab new insoles at REI before we go. Not a big deal.

One more thing — do you want to rent a car or just use rideshare the whole time? I'm leaning toward renting something since we want to do that Runyon Canyon loop and the trails up past Topanga, and Uber surge on a Sunday morning is going to be brutal.
snippet: Flights are booked! Confirmation number is XHQK72, I forwarded the email to you ...
subject: Re: Trip logistics - need to finalize a few things

body:
Got the confirmation, thanks for sending it over. And yes, definitely rent a car — you're right about the surge pricing, and I also just want the flexibility to stop wherever without feeling rushed. Check Costco Travel, they usually have decent rates and it's already on our membership.

Good idea on the insoles. Just get them before Friday so you've had at least a couple days to break them in.

I'll handle packing the snacks and the small first aid kit. Can you be in charge of downloading the offline maps for the Topanga area? Last time we relied on cell service in the hills it was a disaster. We should probably also leave Tara a rough itinerary just so someone knows where we are.
snippet: Got the confirmation, thanks for sending it over. And yes, definitely rent a car...
subject: Re: Trip logistics - need to finalize a few things

body:
Just booked through Costco — got a Subaru Outback for the weekend, which honestly is perfect for the canyon roads. Saved like $40 compared to the other sites too.

Offline maps are downloaded and I made a little itinerary doc I'll share with Tara. I kept it loose but at least she'll know which trails we're targeting each day and where we're staying.

I think we're actually all set. I'm really looking forward to this — we've needed a real trip for a while. Just don't let me overpack this time, I'm going with one bag only.
snippet: Just booked through Costco — got a Subaru Outback for the weekend, which honestl...
subject: Re: Trip logistics - need to finalize a few things

body:
Hey Steph, I finally put together the mood board for the living room redesign. I know we've been going back and forth on the color palette, so I pulled together three options that I think could work for the space.

I shared the folder with you on Google Drive — it has the paint swatches, furniture layouts, and a rough budget breakdown for each option. Option B is my personal favorite but I know you had concerns about the darker tones.

Can you take a look this week and let me know what you think? The interior designer wants a decision by Friday so she can start sourcing pieces.
snippet: Hey Steph, I finally put together the mood board for the living room redesign. I...
subject: Design Review - Living Room Ideas

body:
Hi Matt! Just had a chance to go through the folder — really nice work pulling all of that together. Option B actually grew on me once I saw it with the lighter rug in the layout. The contrast works better than I expected.

My only hesitation is the sectional in that option. It feels a little oversized for the room dimensions. Do you think we could swap it for something closer to what we saw at that showroom in March?

Other than that I'm pretty much sold. Let me know what the designer says about the sofa swap and I think we're good to move forward.
snippet: Hi Matt! Just had a chance to go through the folder — really nice work pulling a...
subject: Re: Design Review - Living Room Ideas

body:
So glad Option B is working for you! I had the same thought about the sectional honestly, I just didn't want to restart the whole furniture search. I reached out to the showroom and they still have the one we liked in stock — the slate gray version. It's actually about $300 less than the one in the mood board, so that's a bonus.

I'll send the updated layout to the designer today with the sofa swap noted. She said as long as we confirm by end of day Thursday we're still on schedule.

Should I just make the call or do you want to review the updated layout first?
snippet: So glad Option B is working for you! I had the same thought about the sectional ...
subject: Re: Design Review - Living Room Ideas

body:
Go ahead and make the call! I trust you on the updated layout — we've been going back and forth long enough and honestly the slate gray sounds perfect.

Just make sure the designer has our delivery address on file. Last time there was a mix-up with the building's freight elevator booking and it was a whole thing.

So excited to finally see this come together. Good work, babe.
snippet: Go ahead and make the call! I trust you on the updated layout — we've been going...
subject: Re: Design Review - Living Room Ideas

body:
Hey, just got a notification that our Hulu plan is up for renewal on the 22nd. It's going to auto-charge the joint card for another year at $89.99 unless we cancel before then.

I know we talked about maybe switching to Netflix since your sister uses it and could add us to her plan. Might save us some money. What do you think — renew Hulu or make the switch?

Let me know before Wednesday so I have time to sort it out.
snippet: Hey, just got a notification that our Hulu plan is up for renewal on the 22nd. I...
subject: Subscription renewal — Hulu or Netflix?

body:
Good catch, I totally forgot about that. Let's go ahead and cancel Hulu before it renews — I'd rather not pay for a full year upfront right now with the wedding stuff coming up.

I'll text Tara today about the Netflix thing. She said she had an open slot on her plan so it should be no problem. That way we're only paying like $5 a month instead.

Can you log in and cancel Hulu? I think the account is under your email. Thanks!
snippet: Good catch, I totally forgot about that. Let's go ahead and cancel Hulu before i...
subject: Re: Subscription renewal — Hulu or Netflix?

body:
Hey! Are you free any day this week for lunch? I feel like it's been forever since we actually sat down together without a million wedding things on the agenda. I just want to eat tacos and gossip like normal people lol.

I'm pretty flexible — Wednesday or Thursday work best for me but I can move things around. Let me know!
snippet: Hey! Are you free any day this week for lunch? I feel like it's been forever sin...
subject: lunch this week??

body:
YES. Absolutely yes, I need this more than you know. This week has been insane and I could really use a taco situation with zero talk of centerpieces or seating charts.

Thursday works perfectly for me! That new place on Clement Street? I think it's called Casa Verde or something — I saw it on Instagram and it looks so good. Like 12:30?
snippet: YES. Absolutely yes, I need this more than you know. This week has been insane a...
subject: Re: lunch this week??

body:
Thursday 12:30 is perfect! And YES I've been wanting to try Casa Verde forever, great call.

I'll make a reservation just in case it gets busy. Can't wait — I have so much to tell you about the whole situation with my coworker drama. It's a lot. Bring your appetite and your patience lol. See you Thursday! 🌮
snippet: Thursday 12:30 is perfect! And YES I've been wanting to try Casa Verde forever, ...
subject: Re: lunch this week??

body:
Hey, just a heads up — you mentioned last night you wanted to send out that weekly status doc to the team by EOD today. Did you get a chance to finish it up? I know you had back-to-back calls this morning so just wanted to make sure it didn't slip through the cracks.

Also, are you still on for dinner at 7? Let me know if you need more time and we can push it back a bit.

Matt
snippet: Hey, just a heads up — you mentioned last night you wanted to send out that week...
subject: Weekly status doc

body:
Oh gosh, thank you for the reminder — it absolutely would have slipped. I just finished the last section and I'm sending it out now. The product review ran way over and I lost track of time completely.

Dinner at 7 still works, I should be wrapped up here by 6:30 at the latest. Can you grab some wine on your way home? The good kind, I've earned it today.

Steph
snippet: Oh gosh, thank you for the reminder — it absolutely would have slipped. I just f...
subject: Re: Weekly status doc

body:
Hey, quick follow-up after I slept on the cutover plan. I'm fine with the Friday parallel run, but can we agree on a hard go/no-go checkpoint at 11pm before we flip anything? If the staging dry run throws even one token-refresh error I'd rather slip a day than fire-fight at midnight.

Also — Devon mentioned the old endpoint still has a couple of internal services pointed at it. Can you confirm those are migrated, or at least flagged, before Saturday? I don't want a surprise dependency taking us down right before the Hartwell launch.

- Matt
